title: Lock scrambles every number — the private key brings each one home
setup:
# --- plot helper (hidden) — RSA lock and unlock for every message ---
# Both curves come from the student's OWN encrypt/decrypt: 'locked' is
# encrypt(m), 'unlocked' is decrypt(encrypt(m)) — which should land back on the
# diagonal. Falls back to built-in pow until encrypt/decrypt work.
def __plot(e, d, n):
    try:
        locked = [[m, encrypt(m, e, n)] for m in range(n)]                 # <- the STUDENT's function
        unlocked = [[m, decrypt(encrypt(m, e, n), d, n)] for m in range(n)]  # <- the STUDENT's functions
        if locked and unlocked and all(isinstance(pt[1], int) for pt in unlocked):
            return [
                {"name": "unlocked with private key (back home, your code)", "points": unlocked, "highlight": True},
                {"name": "after locking with public key", "points": locked, "highlight": False},
            ]
    except Exception:
        pass
    locked = [[m, pow(m, e, n)] for m in range(n)]
    unlocked = [[m, pow(pow(m, e, n), d, n)] for m in range(n)]
    return [
        {"name": "unlocked with private key (back home)", "points": unlocked, "highlight": True},
        {"name": "after locking with public key", "points": locked, "highlight": False},
    ]

caption.todo: Finish encrypt and decrypt to lock and unlock the numbers.
caption.tuning: Your scramble is showing — keep tuning until it matches the target.
caption.match: ✅ Your lock-and-unlock matches the exact answer!
